note: Learners are entered for either Foundation tier (Papers 1, 2, 3) or Higher tier (Papers 4, 5, 6). Foundation tier targets grades 5-1. Higher tier targets grades 9-4.

papers.description: Written paper covering the Foundation tier content. Calculator not permitted.
Written paper covering the Foundation tier content. Calculator permitted.
Written paper covering the Foundation tier content. Calculator permitted.
Written paper covering the Higher tier content. Calculator not permitted.
Written paper covering the Higher tier content. Calculator permitted.
Written paper covering the Higher tier content. Calculator permitted.

assessmentObjectives.name: Solve problems within mathematics and in other contexts

topics.name: Number - Structure and Calculation
subtopics: Order positive and negative integers, decimals and fractions
Use the symbols =, !=, <, >, <=, >=
Apply the four operations to integers, decimals and fractions
Understand and use place value
Understand and use prime numbers, factors, multiples, HCF, LCM
Understand and use systematic listing strategies
Use positive integer powers and associated real roots
Calculate with roots and integer and fractional indices
Calculate exactly with fractions, surds and multiples of pi
Calculate with and interpret standard form A x 10^n

topics.name: Number - Fractions, Decimals and Percentages
subtopics: Work interchangeably with terminating decimals and fractions
Convert between fractions, decimals and percentages
Understand and use equivalent forms

topics.name: Number - Measures and Accuracy
subtopics: Use standard units of measure and related concepts
Use and interpret algebraic notation
Use standard units of mass, length, time, money
Estimate answers and check calculations using approximation and estimation
Round numbers and measures to an appropriate degree of accuracy
Apply and interpret limits of accuracy including upper and lower bounds

topics.name: Algebra - Notation, Vocabulary and Manipulation
subtopics: Use and interpret algebraic notation
Substitute numerical values into formulae and expressions
Understand and use the concepts and vocabulary of expressions, equations, formulae, identities, inequalities, terms and factors
Simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions by collecting like terms
Multiply out brackets and factorise linear and quadratic expressions
Know the difference between an equation and an identity
Use algebraic methods to solve linear equations
Rearrange formulae to change the subject
Argue mathematically to show algebraic expressions are equivalent

topics.name: Algebra - Graphs
subtopics: Work with coordinates in all four quadrants
Plot graphs of equations that correspond to straight-line graphs
Use the form y = mx + c to identify parallel and perpendicular lines
Find the equation of a line through two given points or through one point with given gradient
Identify and interpret gradients and intercepts of linear functions graphically
Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs of linear and quadratic functions
Recognise, sketch and interpret graphs of simple cubic functions, reciprocal functions and exponential functions
Sketch translations and reflections of a given function
Understand and use standard compound interest and growth/decay formulae
Solve linear and quadratic equations using graphs
Estimate solutions to equations using iteration
Estimate areas under graphs using trapezia

topics.name: Algebra - Solving Equations and Inequalities
subtopics: Solve linear equations in one unknown
Solve quadratic equations algebraically by factorising
Solve quadratic equations by using the quadratic formula
Solve quadratic equations by completing the square
Solve two simultaneous equations in two variables
Solve linear inequalities in one or two variables
Represent the solution set to an inequality on a number line and on a graph

topics.name: Algebra - Higher Tier Topics
subtopics: Simplify and manipulate algebraic expressions involving algebraic fractions
Factorise quadratic expressions of the form ax^2 + bx + c
Complete the square for quadratic expressions
Solve quadratic equations including those requiring rearrangement
Solve two simultaneous equations in two variables (linear/quadratic)
Solve linear and quadratic inequalities in one variable
Represent the solution set on a number line and on a graph
Deduce roots and turning points of quadratic functions algebraically
Recognise and use the equation of a circle with centre at the origin
Find the equation of a tangent to a circle at a given point
Use iteration to find approximate solutions to equations
Translate simple situations into algebraic expressions and formulae

topics.name: Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change
topics.category: Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change
subtopics: Change freely between related standard units
Use scale factors, scale diagrams and maps
Express one quantity as a fraction of another
Use ratio notation including reduction to simplest form
Divide a given quantity into two parts in a given part:part or part:whole ratio
Understand and use proportion as equality of ratios
Relate ratios to fractions and to linear functions
Define percentage as 'number of parts per hundred'
Interpret percentages and percentage changes multiplicatively
Express one quantity as a percentage of another
Compare two quantities using percentages
Work with percentages greater than 100%
Solve problems involving percentage change
Solve problems involving direct and inverse proportion
Use compound units such as speed, rates of pay, unit pricing, density and pressure
Compare lengths, areas and volumes using ratio notation

topics.name: Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change - Higher Topics
topics.category: Ratio, Proportion and Rates of Change
subtopics: Interpret the gradient of a straight line graph as a rate of change
Recognise and interpret graphs that illustrate direct and inverse proportion
Interpret the gradient at a point on a curve as instantaneous rate of change
Apply concepts of average and instantaneous rate of change
Set up, solve and interpret growth and decay problems

topics.name: Geometry and Measures - Properties and Constructions
subtopics: Use conventional terms and notation for points, lines, vertices, edges, planes, parallel and perpendicular lines, right angles and polygons
Recall and use properties of angles at a point, on a straight line, vertically opposite angles
Understand and use alternate and corresponding angles on parallel lines
Derive and use the sum of angles in a triangle and in any polygon
Understand and use regular polygons
Construct diagrams using straight edge and compasses
Identify and construct congruent triangles
Apply properties of quadrilaterals including square, rectangle, parallelogram, trapezium, kite and rhombus
Know and use Pythagoras' theorem in 2D
Use trigonometric ratios in right-angled triangles
Know the exact values of sin, cos and tan at key angles
Understand and use bearings

topics.name: Geometry and Measures - Mensuration and Calculation
subtopics: Know and apply formulae to calculate area of triangles, parallelograms, trapezia
Know and apply formulae to calculate volume of cuboids and other right prisms
Know the formulae for circumference and area of a circle
Calculate perimeters of 2D shapes including circles
Calculate areas of circles and composite shapes
Calculate surface area and volume of spheres, pyramids, cones and composite solids
Calculate arc lengths, angles and areas of sectors
Apply congruence and similarity including relationships between lengths, areas and volumes

topics.name: Geometry and Measures - Higher Topics
subtopics: Know and use the trigonometric ratios sine, cosine and tangent in general triangles (sine and cosine rules)
Know and apply Area = 1/2 ab sin C
Know the exact values of sin, cos and tan at 0, 30, 45, 60 and 90 degrees
Know and use the sine and cosine rules and Area = 1/2 ab sin C
Describe translations as 2D vectors
Apply addition and subtraction of vectors
Use vectors to construct geometric arguments and proofs

topics.name: Probability
subtopics: Record and analyse the frequency of outcomes of probability experiments
Apply ideas of randomness, fairness and equally likely events
Relate relative expected frequencies to theoretical probability
Apply the property that probabilities sum to one
Understand that empirical unbiased samples tend towards theoretical probability
Enumerate sets and combinations systematically using tables, grids, Venn diagrams and tree diagrams
Construct possibility spaces for single and combined experiments
Calculate the probability of independent and dependent combined events
Interpret conditional probabilities

topics.name: Probability - Higher Topics
subtopics: Calculate and interpret conditional probabilities
Use Venn diagrams, tree diagrams and two-way tables for conditional probability

topics.name: Statistics - Data Analysis
subtopics: Infer properties of populations or distributions from a sample
Interpret and construct tables, charts and diagrams including frequency tables, bar charts, pie charts, pictograms
Interpret and construct vertical line charts for ungrouped discrete numerical data
Interpret and construct stem and leaf diagrams
Calculate the mean, median, mode and range of ungrouped data
Interpret, analyse and compare the distributions of data sets
Use and interpret scatter graphs
Recognise correlation
Draw estimated lines of best fit
Make predictions and interpolate

topics.name: Statistics - Higher Topics
subtopics: Construct and interpret diagrams for grouped data including histograms and cumulative frequency graphs
Interpret, analyse and compare distributions of data sets via quartiles and interquartile range
Understand outliers and clean data
Understand and use the formal notation for cumulative distribution
